#+TITLE: Architecture Decision Record Template
#+AUTHOR: Project Contributor
#+EMAIL: student@email.edu
#+DATE: <YYYY-MM-DD Day>
#+KEYWORDS: ADR, template
#+DESCRIPTION: Master template for recording architectural and engineering decisions.

#+begin_src org
,#+title: ADR-XXXX: Short Descriptive Title #+date: YYYY-MM-DD #+status: [Proposed | Accepted | Superseded]
#+end_src

* Context

Provide a clear statement of the engineering problem or system requirement. 
Explain the constraints, background information, and specific hardware or mathematical challenges (e.g., ESP32-C6 memory limits, spin-state latency requirements) that necessitate a decision.

* Decision

State the chosen course of action clearly and definitively. Focus on the *what* and the *how* of the implementation strategy.

* Rationale

Detail the explicit engineering reasons behind this choice:
- Detail point 1 (e.g., performance impact on the MCU execution loop).
- Detail point 2 (e.g., simplification of the distributed synchronization protocol).
- Detail point 3 (e.g., alignment with the lab's mathematical model papers).

* Alternatives Considered

List the secondary paths that were evaluated but ultimately passed over.

** [Alternative Title 1]

Explain what this option was and explicitly why it was rejected.

** [Alternative Title 2]

Explain what this option was and explicitly why it was rejected.

* Consequences

Outline the downstream impacts, required follow-up actions, and trade-offs resulting from this decision:
- Action item or technical debt introduced.
- New software modules or parsers that must be written.
- Operational impacts on the cluster performance or hardware wiring layout.
